title: Migration and Immigration in the 1990s and 2000s
description: Test your skills on the causes and effects of domestic and international migration in the late twentieth century!
topic_path: Migration and immigration in the 1990s and 2000s | 9.5
Migration and Immigration in the 1990s and 2000s

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Reagan personified the Sunbelt’s role in the transformation of American politics at the end of the twentieth century. More to the point, Reagan’s presidency, but especially his campaign for the presidency, articulated a hopeful and appealing vision for America — one ostensibly rooted in a reverence for the nation’s revolutionary past, but one that was actually more powerfully organized around the ideals of the postwar Sunbelt. During the 1980s, with Reagan as their popular figurehead, Sunbelt conservatives finally seized control of the national Republican party.” 

> -Source: Sean P. Cunningham, historian, *American Politics in the Postwar Sunbelt: Conservative Growth in a Battleground Region,* 2014

**The excerpt best illustrates which of the following developments in the late twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the political influence of southern and western states
the rise of liberal ideology within the Republican Party
the influence of evangelical organizations on the presidency
the migration of millions of people to states in the Northeast

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Reagan personified the Sunbelt’s role in the transformation of American politics at the end of the twentieth century. More to the point, Reagan’s presidency, but especially his campaign for the presidency, articulated a hopeful and appealing vision for America — one ostensibly rooted in a reverence for the nation’s revolutionary past, but one that was actually more powerfully organized around the ideals of the postwar Sunbelt. During the 1980s, with Reagan as their popular figurehead, Sunbelt conservatives finally seized control of the national Republican party.” 

> -Source: Sean P. Cunningham, historian, *American Politics in the Postwar Sunbelt: Conservative Growth in a Battleground Region,* 2014

**Which of the following most directly led to the changes described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the migration of millions of people to the South and West
the rise in public confidence that the government could fix economic issues
the end of a nearly 40-year-long period of tension with the Soviet Union
the improvements in digital communications technology

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “Reagan personified the Sunbelt’s role in the transformation of American politics at the end of the twentieth century. More to the point, Reagan’s presidency, but especially his campaign for the presidency, articulated a hopeful and appealing vision for America — one ostensibly rooted in a reverence for the nation’s revolutionary past, but one that was actually more powerfully organized around the ideals of the postwar Sunbelt. During the 1980s, with Reagan as their popular figurehead, Sunbelt conservatives finally seized control of the national Republican party.” 

> -Source: Sean P. Cunningham, historian, *American Politics in the Postwar Sunbelt: Conservative Growth in a Battleground Region,* 2014

**One major change in the US from 1970 to 1990 was the Republican Party’s:**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: shift towards a more conservative ideology within the Republican Party.
creation of a coalition of minority voters and Southerners in the Republican Party.
prohibition of voting rights restrictions for African American voters.
reduction of federal spending after the economy stagnated and inflation rose.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “The Sunbelt boom of the late 1970s paralleled the popularization of country music and auto racing; the expansion and relocation of professional sports franchises into the region; and even the country’s mythical love affair with the National Football League’s Dallas Cowboys, dubbed ‘America’s Team’ in 1976. In sum, the Sunbelt became fashionable in the 1970s.”

> -Source: Sean P. Cunningham, historian, *American Politics in the Postwar Sunbelt: Conservative Growth in a Battleground Region,* 2014

**The excerpt best illustrates which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the cultural influence of the southern and western regions
the rise in leisure time as productivity increased
the migration of middle class families to the suburbs
the spread of American culture to other countries

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “The Sunbelt boom of the late 1970s paralleled the popularization of country music and auto racing; the expansion and relocation of professional sports franchises into the region; and even the country’s mythical love affair with the National Football League’s Dallas Cowboys, dubbed ‘America’s Team’ in 1976. In sum, the Sunbelt became fashionable in the 1970s.”

> -Source: Sean P. Cunningham, historian, *American Politics in the Postwar Sunbelt: Conservative Growth in a Battleground Region,* 2014

**The developments described in the excerpt most directly reflect which of the following changes in the late twentieth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the population shift after people migrated to southern and western states
the rise of a shared national culture after the invention of the television
the urbanization of the United States as the economy improved
the increase in cultural clashes between younger and older generations

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the following question.

> “The Sunbelt boom of the late 1970s paralleled the popularization of country music and auto racing; the expansion and relocation of professional sports franchises into the region; and even the country’s mythical love affair with the National Football League’s Dallas Cowboys, dubbed ‘America’s Team’ in 1976. In sum, the Sunbelt became fashionable in the 1970s.”

> -Source: Sean P. Cunningham, historian, *American Politics in the Postwar Sunbelt: Conservative Growth in a Battleground Region,* 2014 

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that migration to the Sunbelt had on the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Sunbelt region influenced America’s cultural identity.
The Sunbelt region emerged as an economic force.
The Sunbelt faced mass immigration from South America.
The Sunbelt ushered in a period of religious conservatism.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2f355bfe3ab9e70806f38b6ca79cb49.svg)

>-Source: [Department of Homeland Security](https://www.dhs.gov/xlibrary/assets/statistics/yearbook/2008/ois_yb_2008.pdf) 

**Which of the following statements best describes the change over time in immigration depicted in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The number of immigrants from Asian and Latin American countries increased.
Immigration from European and Asian countries decreased steadily.
The majority of Asian immigrants relocated to western states like California.
Immigration from African and Latin American countries increased at the fastest rate.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2f355bfe3ab9e70806f38b6ca79cb49.svg)

>-Source: [Department of Homeland Security](https://www.dhs.gov/xlibrary/assets/statistics/yearbook/2008/ois_yb_2008.pdf) 

**Which of the following was a significant cause of the trend from 1960 to 1999 shown in the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the elimination of immigration quotas set decades earlier
the creation of short-term farm contracts for immigrants
the rise in manufacturing jobs as the economy improved
the start of communist revolutions in Latin America and Asia

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2f355bfe3ab9e70806f38b6ca79cb49.svg)

>-Source: [Department of Homeland Security](https://www.dhs.gov/xlibrary/assets/statistics/yearbook/2008/ois_yb_2008.pdf) 

**Which of the following best explains the overall population trend shown in the table?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Asian and Latin American immigration increased dramatically from 1960 to 1999.
European and African immigration decreased rapidly from 1960 to 1999.
Asian and European immigration increased from 1960 to 1980 before rapidly decreasing.
Latin American and African immigration slowly grew from 1960 to 1980 before stalling.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2f355bfe3ab9e70806f38b6ca79cb49.svg)

>-Source: [Department of Homeland Security](https://www.dhs.gov/xlibrary/assets/statistics/yearbook/2008/ois_yb_2008.pdf) 

**Which of the following was a direct effect of the trend in immigration after 1960 shown on the graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: new immigrants influenced and changed American culture
unemployment rose for American citizens in manufacturing
new laws passed by Congress increased barriers to immigration
settlement houses emerged to help immigrants assimilate

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1271cc614a6d784d26062a0b27141447.svg)

>-Source: Elizabeth Grieco, “The Foreign Born in the U.S. Labor Force: Numbers and Trends,” [Migration Policy Institute,](https://www.migrationpolicy.org/research/foreign-born-us-labor-force-numbers-and-trends) 2004

**Which of the following best explains the overall demographic trend shown in the chart?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the growth in the number of immigrants working in American industry
the rise in the number of immigrant members of labor unions
the surge in immigration as the American economy improved
the increase in unemployment as manufacturing jobs dwindled

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1271cc614a6d784d26062a0b27141447.svg)

>-Source: Elizabeth Grieco, “The Foreign Born in the U.S. Labor Force: Numbers and Trends,” [Migration Policy Institute,](https://www.migrationpolicy.org/research/foreign-born-us-labor-force-numbers-and-trends) 2004

**The graph most strongly supports which of the following arguments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Immigrants supplied the economy with a significant number of laborers.
Immigrants did not find many employment opportunities in American industries.
Immigrants mostly worked on farms and not in major industries like manufacturing.
Immigrants had short-term contracts to work, leading to lower participation rates.
